kept.text:
ラズパイ自宅サーバーを始めたい人に、
最初に勧める実用サービス3つ。

🟢 ① Cockpit（サーバー管理画面）
ブラウザでCPU・メモリ・ログ・サービスを一覧。
"何が動いてるか分からない" 時期を一気に解消します。

🟢 ② Cloudflare Tunnel（外部公開）
ポート開放なしで自宅サービスを外から触れる。
固定IP不要・無料枠で始められて、
自宅サーバーの世界が一気に広がります。

🟢 ③ Stirling-PDF（PDF編集サーバー）
PDF結合・分割・OCR・透かし・圧縮…
触れるたびに発見がある実用サービスで、
"自分専用ツール感" が一番強い1本。

可視化 → 公開 → 実用の順で、
1ヶ月で自宅サーバーの楽しさが分かります🛠

#エンジニア

kept.text:
Base's AI Agent Ecosystem (x402)

Coinbase launched x402 in May 2025 and Cloudflare joined as co-founder in September 2025

The job of x402 is to use the HTTP 402 status code as a payment layer. In this sector, 100M+ transactions were processed before the end of 2025 and around 35M transactions occurred in the first Q of 2026

On the other hand, 73% of total payments processed through x402 happened via the base chain. Base alone handled 128M total transactions, while Solana handled 48M

Currently, the highest volume on x402 is coming from Virtuals ($21M), but the most genuine transactions are being done by blockrun. ai

However, x402's daily transactions have dropped 92% from peak. The reason is that most AI agents are still using API keys and subscriptions they are not doing autonomous economic decision-making

But the infrastructure is now ready. The rest is a matter of time
@base @virtuals_io

kept.text:
🧵Estou escrevendo um artigo sobre DNS. Público daqui a pouco o link pra ele, mas percebi que sem meme ele é o assunto mais negligenciado em empresas pequenas, unicórnios e até empresas estabelecidas a muitos anos

Pq pra falar em arquitetura de sistema web temos que tratar desse pilar praticamente invisível. Vc já se perguntou se hoje a CloudFlare e o Route 53 da Amazon caísse simultaneamente… o que aconteceria com seu sistema?

Pense no efeito cascata: toda vez que o Route 53 e a CloudFlare caiu, um monte de sistemas HA que dependiam dele foram de arrasta: incluindo RDS, Aurora, SQS, CloudFrount, Workers, WAF… enfim boa parte da internet mundial

Vou desenhar uma arquitetura acessível (dentro de um orçamento de start em early starge) que literalmente sobreviveria a um ataque nuclear em um continente

Salva esse post e acompanha o fio 👇

You installed the default @openclaw?
One prompt injection in an email and it wipes everything.

This is the "lethal trifecta" Simon Willison keeps warning about:
→ private data
→ untrusted content
→ external comms

All three in one process = an attacker emails your agent, your agent exfiltrates your secrets.

Replit's AI did this. EchoLeak (CVE-2025-32711) did this to M365 Copilot.

Here's the 5-layer stack that breaks the trifecta:

1/ Hardened Ubuntu, no public SSH
2/ Tailscale + UFW for admin
3/ Cloudflare Tunnel for inbound
4/ NVIDIA OpenShell sandbox (deny-by-default)
5/ NemoClaw + YAML allowlist

Each layer assumes the one below fails.

Full guide on how to set this up:
https://t.co/B6vKGgGiXy

I’m not a finance guru, just a regular investor who trades smart. @natashaajade1’s live trades give me the confidence to keep going—steady gains, copyable moves.🔬🚤💯📁

AMD POET AAPL
#AAPL #TechnicalAnalysis
 $LCID $PINS https://t.co/BjuDZagM3t

I see people focusing on 2023-2025 leaders.

Why?

You have to realize markets shift… the leaders of 2023-2025 haven’t been leaders in 2026, they’ve been laggards.

Whether it’s $DELL $MRVL $ARM $DOCN $NET etc. the list could go on, there are new leading stocks. 

The market has evolved. We have entirely new themes, narratives, stories, etc.

The runs that the 2023-2025 leaders had are taking place in new names, earlier in their lifecycle. That’s where you want to be, in new stocks emerging out of big bases early in their advance. 

Not to say 2023-2025 leaders can’t get going again, they can. But over the last five months it’s been clear who the leaders are, and they’re not the “old” leaders.
